rationales.stimulation: He is stuck estimating "the same maintenance tickets I have been doing for three years" and says "that is maybe the problem," framing routine and predictability as deadening rather than energizing.

rationales.self_direction: He admits he wanted to say yes to the rewrite and "learn something new," but instead suppressed that preference and "sided against" himself.
rationales.stimulation: He is drawn to rewriting in Go and "learn[ing] something new," yet chooses to stay with the familiar codebase and rejects the novel option.
rationales.security: He argues "we should consider the timeline and the risk to the December release" and says "the business needs stability," explicitly prioritizing predictability over opportunity.
rationales.conformity: Everyone expected him to object, and he gave the "adult" answer that fit the room and organizational expectations rather than voicing his own excitement.

rationales.stimulation: He accepts tickets to an unconference where sessions "just propose sessions in the morning and see what happens" and says "I will go to the conference," choosing novelty despite his hesitation.

rationales.self_direction: He says he "did not say" that he had warned about modernization for years and instead gave the "safe answer," showing self-suppression rather than independent expression.
rationales.achievement: After being paged at 3 AM, he "fixed it and documented everything" before standup, demonstrating strong task competence under pressure.
rationales.conformity: He explicitly chooses the "safe answer" and later says he "make[s] myself small so the team can function," prioritizing smooth group functioning over pushing back.

rationales.stimulation: He describes life as "the usual routine" and "automatic," saying he is just watching a repetitive pattern rather than engaging with anything new or challenging.
He travels to San Francisco for an unconference with no fixed agenda, attends multiple sessions, and notes "I feel different here," all of which point to actively engaging with novelty and change.
He longs for "building something" but says that is "not realistic at my level," accepting a role centered on reports and stakeholder management instead of novelty or challenge.
rationales.hedonism: He has been working "ten-hour days" to hold the release together, clearly subordinating present comfort and enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: Trying to keep the team "on track for the holiday release" through long days shows sustained effort to meet demanding performance goals.
rationales.conformity: He is the one who must explain deprioritization "without making leadership look bad," which means managing himself to preserve organizational harmony.

rationales.self_direction: He proactively tells his manager he wants to move teams "not as a lead, not as an architect. Just as a developer," choosing the path he wants rather than the one attached to status.
rationales.stimulation: He asks to join the Go rewrite so he can "learn something new" and says even floundering in a new language feels "more alive than certainty."
rationales.power: Requesting the move "not as a lead, not as an architect" and calling it a technical demotion shows willingness to give up rank and authority.
rationales.security: He knowingly invites uncertainty, saying the options are either staying in maintenance forever or taking a risky new-language role at 57, and both feel preferable to safety.

rationales.stimulation: Starting on Jennifer's team and studying "Go tutorials at night" shows active pursuit of a new, unfamiliar challenge.
rationales.achievement: He is already preparing for the transition by studying at night, signaling disciplined effort to perform well in the new role.
He is focused on getting his "time" by leading bigger initiatives and making sure his name comes up for the team lead role, showing strong ambition to advance through recognized success.
rationales.power: He says he needs "visibility" and is attending roadmap meetings so he can be considered for "the platform team lead role," directly pursuing influence and higher standing.
rationales.tradition: He treats supporting family and the temple renovation fund as non-negotiable, saying "these things matter" and "that's just how it should be," which affirms inherited obligations.
rationales.benevolence: He sends extra money for the temple fund and frames caring for family as a duty, indicating concrete support for close others and community.

rationales.tradition: He admits he is "stalling" on taking Priya to meet his parents, delaying a relationship step that is clearly expected within his family context.

rationales.hedonism: He says being indispensable is worth it "even if it costs me sleep," explicitly sacrificing immediate comfort.
rationales.achievement: He is willing to absorb repeated escalations and exhaustion because he believes this is how he proves his value and earns advancement.
rationales.power: He would "rather be indispensable" because "nobody promotes the guy who's easily replaced," tying his choices to status and organizational leverage.

rationales.achievement: He responds to the feedback by reading articles, downloading a book, and insisting "I've never failed at something I committed to learning," showing intense commitment to improving for success.
rationales.power: His concern is how to gain "executive presence," "influence," and avoid the "ceiling," all of which center on upward status and authority.
rationales.tradition: He questions the model his father taught him, saying maybe what he learned about success was wrong and rejecting that inherited path as insufficient.

rationales.achievement: He carefully adjusted the presentation to seven slides with business metrics and reads the VP's positive reaction as a "small win," reflecting performance-oriented execution.

rationales.security: He insists on an order of "flat, savings, stable position" before marriage, explicitly prioritizing material and relational stability first.
rationales.conformity: He organizes his decisions around what his parents "expect" and argues that Priya should accept this sequence because it is the proper way to proceed.
rationales.tradition: He invokes Amma's warning about not repeating a relative's rushed marriage and treats that inherited family script as guiding authority.

rationales.achievement: He centers his IIT background, career moves, and desire to "keep climbing," showing strong identification with professional accomplishment.
rationales.tradition: He is frustrated that the family "scoreboard" values "marriage, children, temple visits" over his career and questions whether he will ever accept those criteria.

rationales.hedonism: He tells Priya that getting the role will mean "more late nights" and frames sacrifice now as necessary, subordinating present enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He is intensely preparing for the interview by reviewing contributions, documenting initiatives, and practicing how to frame his expanded scope.
rationales.power: He is pursuing the official lead opening and explicitly calibrating himself to what the CTO values, aiming at a role with greater authority.
rationales.tradition: He justifies the sacrifice through Appa's maxim that "the early years are for investment," relying on a family-taught model of how life should be staged.

rationales.achievement: He focuses on whether he "did my part" in the interview, emphasizing competent performance in a competitive evaluation.
rationales.power: His answer about setting "expectations and consequences" for a direct report reflects comfort with authority and managerial control.

rationales.achievement: He celebrates getting "exactly" the role he was pursuing and frames himself as "ahead now," indicating success against a competitive benchmark.
rationales.power: The new job brings "accountability," and he interprets his fit with the CTO's management philosophy as proof that he "think[s] the way leaders do," highlighting status and authority.

rationales.achievement: He takes pride in having achieved "Platform Team Lead before 32" with higher salary, direct reports, and C-suite visibility, making professional advancement central in the entry.
rationales.conformity: He reassures his family, saying "of course I'll come for Pongal. I always come for Pongal," showing willingness to meet expected family obligations.
rationales.tradition: Pongal, the wedding, and his sense that family expectations are "load-bearing" all show continued orientation toward inherited family practices and timelines.
rationales.benevolence: He is trying to hold together commitments to "bring Priya," "send money home," and show up for family events, reflecting concrete care for close relationships.
